questions.text: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is approximately 6,650 km long.

questions.text: The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert in Africa is the largest hot desert.

questions.text: Mount Everest is located in which mountain range?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border.

questions.text: Match countries with their capitals:
questions.explanation: These are the official capital cities of these nations.

questions.text: How many continents are there?
questions.explanation: The seven continents are: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, South America.

questions.text: The Pacific Ocean is the smallest ocean on Earth.
questions.explanation: The Pacific is the largest ocean, while the Arctic is the smallest.

questions.text: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline (1,600+ km) among Indian states.

questions.text: Which of these are island nations? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Iceland, Japan, Philippines, and New Zealand are island nations. Spain is mostly peninsular.

questions.text: Assertion: The Equator is the longest latitude line. Reason: It divides Earth into Northern and Southern hemispheres.
questions.explanation: The Equator is indeed the longest parallel, and it does divide the hemispheres.

questions.text: The Grand Canyon is located in which US state?
questions.explanation: The Grand Canyon is in Arizona, carved by the Colorado River.

questions.text: Which is the smallest country in the world by land area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is an independent city-state enclaved within Rome, Italy, and is the smallest country by area.

questions.text: Mount Kilimanjaro is the highest peak in Africa.
questions.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro is a dormant volcano in Tanzania and the highest mountain in Africa.

questions.text: The imaginary line that divides the Earth into the Eastern and Western hemispheres is known as the Prime ______.
questions.explanation: The Prime Meridian is the line of 0 degrees longitude, which passes through Greenwich, England.

questions.text: Which of the following are part of the Great Lakes of North America? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The Great Lakes include Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, and Ontario. Victoria is in Africa and Baikal is in Russia.

questions.text: Assertion: Ocean tides are a recurring natural phenomenon. Reason: The gravitational pull of the Moon and the Sun affects Earth's oceans.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true, and the gravitational pull is the direct cause of the ocean tides.

questions.text: Match the mountain ranges to their respective continents:
questions.explanation: The Andes run through South America, the Alps through Europe, and the Rockies through North America.

questions.text: What is the capital city of Canada?
questions.explanation: While Toronto and Vancouver are larger cities, Ottawa is the capital of Canada.

questions.text: Greenland is recognized as a fully independent sovereign nation.
questions.explanation: Greenland is an autonomous territory within the Kingdom of Denmark.

questions.text: The science or practice of drawing and creating maps is called ______.
questions.explanation: Cartography is the study and practice of making maps, combining science, aesthetics, and technique.

questions.text: Which of these countries are located in South America? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Brazil, Argentina, and Chile are in South America. Spain is in Europe, and Mexico is in North America.

questions.text: Assertion: Deserts are always characterized by extremely hot temperatures. Reason: Antarctica is classified as a desert because it receives very little precipitation.
questions.explanation: Deserts are defined by low precipitation, not temperature. Therefore, the cold continent of Antarctica is the largest desert in the world.

questions.text: Match the major rivers to the countries they primarily flow through:
questions.explanation: The Ganges is central to India, the Seine flows through Paris, France, and the Thames runs through London, UK.

questions.text: Which country is the largest in the world by total land area?
questions.explanation: Russia spans Eastern Europe and Northern Asia and is the largest country by landmass.

questions.text: The Amazon rainforest produces roughly 20 percent of the Earth's oxygen.
questions.explanation: It's a common myth; the Amazon consumes almost all the oxygen it produces. Most of Earth's oxygen comes from marine plants like phytoplankton.

questions.text: The Great Barrier Reef, the world's largest coral reef system, is located off the coast of ______.
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is situated in the Coral Sea, off the coast of Queensland, Australia.

questions.text: Which of the following are Scandinavian countries? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Norway, Sweden, and Denmark make up Scandinavia. Italy and Greece are in Southern Europe.

questions.text: Assertion: Global sea levels have been rising over the past century. Reason: Global warming causes polar ice caps and glaciers to melt, adding water to the oceans.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true, and the melting of ice due to global warming directly explains the rising sea levels.

questions.text: Match the deserts to their respective continents:
questions.explanation: The Gobi is in China and Mongolia, the Kalahari is in Southern Africa, and the Atacama is in Chile.

questions.text: Which is the longest continental mountain range in the world?
questions.explanation: The Andes stretch for over 7,000 km along the western edge of South America.

questions.text: The Dead Sea is known for having an incredibly high salt concentration.
questions.explanation: The Dead Sea is a salt lake bordered by Jordan and Israel, and its high salinity makes it easy to float.

questions.text: A narrow strip of water connecting two larger bodies of water is called a ______.
questions.explanation: Examples include the Strait of Gibraltar or the Strait of Hormuz.

questions.text: Which of the following Indian states share a land border with Pakistan? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Punjab border Pakistan. Kerala and Tamil Nadu are in southern India.

questions.text: Assertion: The Earth experiences different seasons throughout the year. Reason: The Earth's axis of rotation is tilted relative to its orbital plane around the Sun.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct, and the axial tilt is the fundamental reason for the changing seasons.

questions.text: Match the famous landmarks to the continents where they are located:
questions.explanation: These are iconic structures located on their respective continents.

questions.text: What is the capital city of New Zealand?
questions.explanation: Wellington is the capital, although Auckland is the most populous city.

questions.text: The Tropic of Cancer passes almost halfway through the landmass of India.
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through eight Indian states, dividing the country into roughly two equal parts.

questions.text: A piece of land surrounded by water on three sides is called a ______.
questions.explanation: Examples include the Iberian Peninsula, the Florida Peninsula, and the Indian Peninsula.

questions.text: Which of these countries border the Mediterranean Sea? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Italy, Greece, and Egypt border the Mediterranean. Japan is in the Pacific, and Brazil borders the Atlantic.

questions.text: Assertion: The 'Ring of Fire' is an area with a high frequency of earthquakes and volcanic eruptions. Reason: It is located along the edges of the Atlantic Ocean basin.
questions.explanation: The assertion is true, but the reason is false. The Ring of Fire surrounds the Pacific Ocean basin, not the Atlantic.

questions.text: Match the European countries to their capital cities:
questions.explanation: These are major Western European nations and their historic capital cities.

questions.text: Lake Baikal, known for its depth and age, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Lake Baikal is a massive ancient rift lake located in southern Siberia, Russia.

questions.text: Mount Vesuvius is an active volcano located in mainland Europe.
questions.explanation: Mount Vesuvius, famous for destroying Pompeii, is located near Naples, Italy.

questions.text: The lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere, where nearly all weather conditions take place, is the ______.
questions.explanation: The troposphere starts at the Earth's surface and contains most of the atmosphere's mass.

questions.text: Which of the following are landlocked countries? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Nepal, Switzerland, and Chad have no coastline. Australia and Japan are island nations.

questions.text: Assertion: Tsunamis are massive tidal waves caused by the gravitational pull of the moon. Reason: Underwater earthquakes or volcanic eruptions generate tsunamis by displacing huge volumes of water.
questions.explanation: Tsunamis are not tidal waves and are not caused by the moon. They are generated by seismic activity, making the reason true and the assertion false.

questions.text: Match the Indian states to their capital cities:
questions.explanation: These are major Indian states corresponding to their capital megacities.

questions.text: The Mariana Trench, the deepest oceanic trench on Earth, is located in which ocean?
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench is located in the western Pacific Ocean.

questions.text: Vatican City is entirely surrounded by the city of Rome.
questions.explanation: Vatican City is an independent city-state enclaved within Rome, Italy.

questions.text: Molten rock generated by geothermal energy beneath the Earth's surface is called ______.
questions.explanation: Once magma erupts onto the surface, it is then referred to as lava.

questions.text: Which of the following are official languages of the United Nations? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The six official languages are Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, and Spanish.
